_doc: §8.4a.21 W3 analog seed bibliography. Authoritative texts whose causal frameworks frequently appear as analogs in fundamentals-learning modules. S2.5 (analog retrieval) starts here and may expand via web search per the 4-criterion rubric. Curation pass: Cowork browsing-assist + Campbell review per dispatch §8.4a.21 W3.
_revision: v0.1 starter — Code-scaffolded 2026-05-17; needs Cowork bibliography expansion + Campbell review per dispatch §8.4a.21 W3.

entries.author: Joseph Schumpeter
entries.framework_summary: Innovation drives the entrepreneurial process of replacing existing structures; market dynamics constantly recombine and displace prior arrangements.

entries.author: Clayton Christensen
entries.framework_summary: Incumbents serve mainstream customers efficiently but get displaced from below by lower-performance entrants whose price/access trajectories overtake the incumbent over time.
entries.epistemic_notes: Subject to substantial pushback (Jill Lepore 2014, Andrew King + Baljir Baatartogtokh 2015 etc.). Use carefully; flag the contestation in rationale.

entries.author: Carlota Perez
entries.framework_summary: Long-wave (~50yr) cycles of technological revolutions paired with financial-capital phases (installation → frenzy → turning point → synergy → maturity).
entries.epistemic_notes: Widely cited; framework is heuristic rather than fully validated. Strong on infrastructural-tech transitions.

entries.author: Joel Mokyr
entries.framework: Sources of technological creativity in industrial revolutions
entries.framework_summary: Macro-conditions enabling sustained technological progress: micro-inventions accumulating, propositional vs. prescriptive knowledge, institutional + cultural prerequisites.

entries.author: Daniel Yergin
entries.work: The Prize: The Epic Quest for Oil, Money & Power
entries.framework_summary: How control over oil reshaped 20th-century geopolitics, corporate concentration, and state behavior; multi-actor strategic-resource dynamics.

entries.author: Walter Isaacson
entries.framework_summary: Internet + personal computing emerged from specific team structures, government-academia-industry partnerships, and individual collaborations.
entries.epistemic_notes: Useful for ecosystem-formation narratives but lighter on causal rigor than Mokyr or Yergin.

entries.author: Richard Tedlow
entries.work: New and Improved: The Story of Mass Marketing in America
entries.framework_summary: How U.S. consumer markets passed through fragmentation, mass unification (1920s-60s), and re-segmentation; categorical patterns in market structure.

expansion_workflow: Cowork: identify additional authoritative texts per dispatch (e.g. classic monetary history, military doctrine evolution, regulatory capture, network effects). Add entries via PR or direct edit; Campbell reviews at next curation pass.
_curation_note: Initial 7 entries match dispatch §8.4a.21 v0.3 list verbatim. Cowork's W3 browsing-assist pass should expand domain coverage (notable gaps: monetary history, network-economics theory, military procurement history).
